texto: There's always women who show up who... they don't really want a baby, you know? They're just lonely. And you kind of feel like a gigolo here. You're supposed to look good. Charm the women, make them feel comfortable. You just don't have sex with them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kim Golden: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “There's always women who show up who... they don't really want a baby, you know? They're just lon…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, mads, maybe-baby pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de maybe-baby e mingles.

frase_original: There's always women who show up who... they don't really want a baby, you know? They're just lonely. And you kind of feel like a gigolo here. You're supposed to look good. Charm the women, make them feel comfortable. You just don't have sex with them.
